calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss The Action On The Gorund Of Inconvenient Forum
Off Calendar Apr-26-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT SPENCER SMITH'S Motion To Stay Or Dismiss The Action On The Gorund Of Inconvenient Forum is treated is a motion to change venue and GRANTED. No opposition filed. The court transfers this action to Orange County pursuant to the venue selection clause. Plaintiff Mea Forensic to pay the requisite transfer fees. Written order is signed and filed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: torreanom@aol.org (L&M302)

MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DOW PATTEN, SPENCER SMITH's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND VACATE DEFAULT JUDGMENT.

Defendants Spencer Smith and Dow Patten's motion to set aside the January 19, 2017 default and the July 5, 2017 default judgment against them is granted as to Mr. Smith and denied as to Mr. Patten. The only bases for the motion are the mandatory and discretionary provisions of CCP 473(b). The discretionary relief provisions require that a motion to vacate a default and default judgment be brought within six months of the entry of default. Default was entered against the moving defendants more than six months before the motion was filed and, even if the motion was filed less than six months after entry of default judgment, setting aside the default judgment while retaining the default is an idle act and requires that the motion be denied. (Pulte Homes Corporation v. Williams Mechanical, Inc. (2016) 2 Cal. App. 5th 267, 273). The mandatory relief provisions do not apply to Mr. Patten because he was representing himself (Esther B. v. City of Los Angeles (2008) 158 Cal. App. 4th 1093, 1099, but do apply to Mr. Smith, even though no explanation was provided for Mr. Patten's fault in allowing the default and default judgment to be entered (Martin Potts & Associates, Inc. (2016) 244 Cal. App. 4th 432, 438-4) and the motion was made less than six months after entry of default judgment. (Sugasawara v. Newland (1994) 27 Cal. App. 4th 294, 297).

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Nov-15-2017 Off Calendar
